source: Optimal catalogue from __OLD__/data/config/cs1/optimal_qn_model.csv and __OLD__/data/config/cs1/optimal_dim_variables.csv. Baseline (dflt) routing and swap-source services (MAS_3, AS_3, DS_3) carried over from __OLD__/data/config/cs1/default_*.csv for scenario s1.
notes: Three after-adaptation scenarios. The `artifacts` block carries both dflt-variant and opti-variant entries for the three swap slots (MAS_3 vs MAS_4 at node 6, AS_3 vs AS_4 at node 9, DS_3 vs DS_1 at node 11). Each scenario's `_nodes` list names the 13 artifact keys active for that scenario, in routing-matrix order.
TODO_revisit_rates: REVISIT mu (service rates) and lambda_z (arrival rate) before locking the comparison. Same concern as dflt.json: Camara/Weyns numbers (mu in [150, 1580] req/s, lambda_z=345) come from a Java/ReSeP stack; the FastAPI prototype caps at ~200 req/s sustained. The opti swap services (MAS_4 mu=880, AS_4 mu=210, DS_1 mu=250) need the same review as the dflt slots. Whatever rescaling is chosen for dflt.json must be applied symmetrically here so the s1/s2/aggregate scenarios remain comparable. Decide before running 06-yoly-experimental.ipynb at scale.

c_{TAS_{1}}.description: Number of resources allocated in TAS_1

K_{TAS_{1}}.description: Allocated request capacity in TAS_1 memory buffer

M_{act_{TAS_{1}}}.description: Active memory used by processing requests in TAS_{1} (M_act = L * delta).

M_{buf_{TAS_{1}}}.description: Allocated buffer memory for the TAS_{1} queue (M_buf = K * delta).

L_{TAS_{1}}.description: Average number of request in TAS_1 component

L_{q, TAS_{1}}.description: Average number of request in TAS_1 memory

W_{TAS_{1}}.description: Average time to complete a request in TAS_1

W_{q, TAS_{1}}.description: Average time for a request to transit the buffer in TAS_1

d_{TAS_{1}}.description: Data density for request in TAS_1 expected data per requests

_labels.s1: S1 (Retry strategy). Service catalogue: dflt (MAS_3, AS_3, DS_3 at the swap slots). Routing: dflt baseline dispatch (uniform 0.33 across each equivalent pool). The retry mechanism is encoded as a feedback edge from each failure-prone atomic back to its composite dispatcher (MAS_3 -> TAS_2 = eps_MAS_3 = 0.18; AS_3 -> TAS_3 = eps_AS_3 = 0.18; DS_3 -> TAS_4 = eps_DS_3 = 0.05). Failed calls re-enter the composite and are re-dispatched uniformly across the equivalent set. self-loops at MAS_3 / AS_3 / DS_3 are 0; forward edges remain at 1 - eps.
_labels.s2: S2 (Select-Reliable strategy). Service catalogue: opti (MAS_3 -> MAS_4, AS_3 -> AS_4, DS_3 -> DS_1, all values from [13] Weyns & Iftikhar 2016 Table I). Routing: inverse-epsilon-weighted dispatch at the composite stages, biasing each equivalent's invocation probability proportionally to 1/eps (TAS_2 over MAS pool: 0.25 / 0.44 / 0.31 for MAS_1 / MAS_2 / MAS_4 with eps = 0.12 / 0.07 / 0.10; TAS_3 over AS pool: 0.19 / 0.54 / 0.27 for AS_1 / AS_2 / AS_4 with eps = 0.11 / 0.04 / 0.08). Self-loops at the swapped atomics equal their upgraded eps (MAS_4 = 0.10, AS_4 = 0.08, DS_1 = 0.01); forward edges = 1 - eps. No retry feedback.
_labels.aggregate: Aggregate (both S1 and S2 active). Service catalogue: opti (MAS_4, AS_4, DS_1 at the swap slots). Routing: inverse-epsilon-weighted dispatch (MAS pool 0.25 / 0.44 / 0.31; AS pool 0.19 / 0.54 / 0.27) PLUS retry feedback edges from each upgraded swap-slot atomic back to its composite dispatcher. MAS_4 -> TAS_2 = eps_MAS_4 = 0.10; AS_4 -> TAS_3 = eps_AS_4 = 0.08; DS_1 -> TAS_4 = eps_DS_1 = 0.01. Self-loops at the swap-slot atomics are 0; forward edges = 1 - eps. Two adaptation mechanisms compose: failed calls are re-dispatched via composite feedback, and the first-attempt dispatch is biased toward the more reliable equivalent.

environments._note: `_setpoint` names the default scenario for this config — the loader reads this scenario when no `--adaptation` CLI flag is given. `_routs[scenario][i][j]` = routing probability from node i to node j under `scenario`. Position i indexes into `_nodes[scenario]`. `_nodes[scenario][i]` names the artifact at position i for that scenario.
environments._fixme_strategies: src/experimental/prototype/controller/strategies.py: PreferReliablePicker currently picks argmin observed failure rate (Dirac on the lowest-eps service). To match the analytic encoding for S2 / aggregate, replace argmin with an inverse-epsilon weighted sample: weights = (1/eps_i) / sum(1/eps_j) over the candidate set. Same change to RetryAndPreferReliablePicker for aggregate (ranked chain becomes inverse-eps weighted samples without replacement). Note(2026-05-13): pending implementation.
